content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 16, 1989

Honorable Evlyn Wilcox
Mayor
City of San Bernardino
300 North D Street
San Bernardino, CA 92418

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-89-141

Dear Mayor Wilcox:

You have requested advice concerning the application of the
mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act") 1/
to the publication of a notice for free curb-side cleanup from the
mayor's office. This letter confirms that your letter dated
February 27, 1989, accurately summarizes the telephone advice I
provided to you on February 14, 1989.

The cleanup notice may be sent at public expense on the
mayor's letterhead provided any references to elected city
officials are removed from the text of the notice.

Please contact me at (916) 322-5901 if you have any further
questions regarding this matter.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:JWW:plh

_______________________________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

CITY OF

San Bernardino

OFFICE OF THE MAYOR
EVLYN WILCOX
MAYOR

ATTENTION!
"BAKER" AREA RESIDENTS

**FREE**
ONE DAY ONLY!!
CURB-SIDE CLEANUP

Saturday, March 11, 1989
8:00 a.m. - to 12 Noon

If you live within the shaded area of the map, take this opportunity to get rid of old furniture, appliances, refrigerators, wood, metal and plastic--all junk, and debris. Place it at the curb for FREE PICKUP. Exception: NO AUTOMOBILE PARTS.
The Code Enforcement Division will be surveying your neighborhood for health and safety problems and public nuisances after the clean-up day. Should the property on which you live have a problem of health and safety or be a public nuisance, you will be asked to clean it at your own cost. Please take advantage of this free clean-up day.

A CLEAN COMMUNITY
IS
A SAFE COMMUNITY

Contact Leonard Chaves, your Neighborhood Watch Representative if you have any questions at 889-2593 or the Police Community Center at 384-5771.

([illegible] mayor & councilman [illegible] prc [illegible])

PRIDE
IN PROGRESS

300 NORTH D STREET SAN BERNARDINO,
CALIFORNIA 92418-0001 714/384-5133

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 6, 1989


Honorable Evlyn Wilcox
Mayor
City of San Bernardino
300 North "D" Street
San Bernardino, CA  92418-0001

Re:  Letter No. 89-141

Dear Mayor Wilcox:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on March 2, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission.  If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact John Wallace an attorney in the Legal Division,
directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice.  If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to
information needed.  If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can.  (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,


Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

DMG:plh


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

CITY OF

San Bernardino

OFFICE OF THE MAYOR

EVLYN WILCOX
MAYOR

February 27, 1989


Mr.John Wallace
Counsel, Legal Services
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Wallace:

On Tuesday, February 14, 1989, Gregory Garcia of my office
contacted you regarding a clean-up flyer (see enclosed flyer)
which is to be circulated to over 500 households.

At that time you informed him that the flyer as he read it to
you, was perfectly within the legal restrictions of
Proposition #73, as long as the reference to "Mayor Evlyn
Wilcox and Councilman Jack Reilly" were eliminated.
Additionally, you informed him that this notice, printed on
City Letterhead, Office of the Mayor, was not illegal under
Proposition #73, but was in fact legal according to
Subsection "e" of Regulation 18901.

As per your suggestion, I am requesting a "Letter of Advice"
on this matter using the enclosed revised flyer, which
eliminated reference to "Mayor Wilcox and Councilman Jack
Reilly", but remains on City Letterhead, Office of the Mayor.

Thank you for your help in this matter, and I will await your
reply in approximately 21 days.

Sincerely,

Evlyn Wilcox, Mayor

Saturday, March 11, 1989
8:00 a.m. - to 12 Noon

If you live within the shaded area of the map, take this opportunity to get rid of old
furniture, appliances, refrigerators, wood, metal and plastic—all junk, and debris.
Place it at the curb for FREE PICKUP. Exception: NO AUTOMOBILE PARTS.
The Code Enforcement Division will be surveying your neighborhood for health and
safety problems and public nuisances after the clean-up day. Should the property on
which you live have a problem of health and safety or be a public nuisance, you will
be asked to clean it at your own cost. Please take advantage of this free clean-up day.

A CLEAN COMMUNITY
IS
A SAFE COMMUNITY

Contact Leonard Chaves, your Neighborhood Watch Representative if you have any
questions at 889-2593 or the Police Community Center at 384-5771.

[handwritten signature and text: illegible]

PRIDE
IN PROGRESS

300 NORTH D STREET, SAN BERNARDINO,
CALIFORNIA 92418-0001 714/384-5133

CITY OF

San Bernardino

OFFICE OF THE MAYOR

EVLYN WILCOX
MAYOR

ATENCION!
RESIDENTES DE LA AREA "BAKER"

**GRATIS**
UN DIA SOLAMENTE!!
ELIMINE SUS DESPOJOS

SABADO, MARZO 11, 1989
8:00 AM - 12:00 (medio dia)

Si usted vive entre la area sombreado del mapa, tome esta oportunidad para librarse de muebles viejos, refrigeradores, madera, metal, y plastico —todo basura y despojos. Pongalos en la acera en frente de su casa para que se recojo gratis. Excepcion: PARTES DE AUTOMOVILES.

La Division de ejecucion de codigo de la cuidad de San Bernardino estara inspeccionando su vecindad para problemas de salud y seguridad y molestias publicas despues del dia de limpieza. Si la propiedad en cual vive tene un programa de salud, seguridad o molestia publica, sera usted reponsable para limpiarlo a su costo. Por favor tome ventaja de este gratis dia de limpieza.

UNA COMMUNIDAD LIMPA
ES
UNA COMMUNIDAD SEGURA

Comunicase con el Director del Area de Barrio Vigilante, Leonard Chavez, si usted tiene preguntas o necesidad de asistencia, 889-2593.

300 NORTH D STREET SAN BERNARDINO
CALIFORNIA 92418 0001 714/384-5133

PRIDE
IN PROCESS

CITY OF

San Bernardino

OFFICE OF THE MAYOR

EVLYN WILCOX
MAYOR

February 27, 1989


Mr.John Wallace
Counsel, Legal Services
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Wallace:

On Tuesday, February 14, 1989, Gregory Garcia of my office

At that time you informed him that the flyer as he read it to

As per your suggestion, I am requesting a "Letter of Advice"

Thank you for your help in this matter, and I will await your

Sincerely,

Evlyn Wilcox, Mayor
